docs.title: Welcome to English x Bancie! ❤️
docs.text:
Hey IELTS Crew!

Welcome to your ultimate IELTS hangout. Here, I've gathered all my favorite resources, tips, and tricks that helped me crush the test. Whether you're aiming for a top score or just getting started, this place has got you covered. Dive in, explore, and let's nail those IELTS goals together!

docs.title: Web mapping
docs.text:
• Home
  • Notebook
    • Writing
    • Speaking
    • Reading
    • Listening
  • Synonyms
    • Tabular
    • Topic
  • Word form
    • Tabular
  • Practical

Hope this captures the natural and friendly tone you're looking for!

docs.title: FULL TESTS - tuhocielts.dolenglish.vn
docs.text: This link provides access to free IELTS online tests, enabling learners to practice and prepare for the IELTS exam. It includes various sections such as Listening, Reading, Writing, and Speaking to offer a comprehensive practice experience.

docs.title: WRITING - zim.vn
docs.text: This page offers a compilation of the latest IELTS Writing tests for 2023, complete with sample answers. It helps learners understand the current trends and difficulty levels of the exam, providing a valuable resource for effective preparation.

docs.title: SPEAKING - ome.tv
docs.text: OME.tv is a platform that connects users via video chat, offering an excellent opportunity for IELTS Speaking practice. Users can engage in real-time conversations with people from around the world, improving their speaking skills and gaining confidence.

docs.title: READING - tuhocielts.dolenglish.vn
docs.text: This link leads to a collection of IELTS Reading practice materials. It includes various passages and questions designed to simulate the actual IELTS Reading test, helping learners enhance their reading comprehension and test-taking strategies.

docs.title: LISTENING - tuhocielts.dolenglish.vn
docs.text: Here, learners can find a range of IELTS Listening practice exercises. These resources provide realistic listening tasks similar to those found in the IELTS exam, aiding in the development of listening skills and familiarity with the test format.

docs.title: LISTENING DICTATION - dailydictation.com
docs.text: Daily Dictation offers exercises aimed at improving listening and dictation skills. This site provides various audio recordings along with transcription tasks, helping learners sharpen their ability to understand spoken English and improve their spelling and grammar.

docs.title: Actions and Processes
docs.text:
• Use: consume
  • Earn: gain
  • Release: emit, discharge
  • Participate in: join, engage in, take part in
  • Invest in: allocate for, spent on
  • Construct: build, erect, establish
  • Apply: exert
  • Modify: change, adjust, alter, amend, shift
  • Nurture: foster, upbringing, care
  • Work with: cooperate, collaborate with

docs.title: Feelings and Qualities
docs.text:
• Ease: alleviate, allay, assuage, mitigate
  • Unfavorable: adverse, negative, unfortunate, unfriendly
  • Instinctive: deep-seated, elemental, inborn, ingrained, inherent, innate, intrinsic, intuitive
  • Surprising: unexpected, amazing, unbelievable, shocking, unpredictable, incredible, astonishing, astounding, crazy
  • Perfect: flawless, impeccable
  • Wonderful: marvelous, fantastic, terrific
  • Important: crucial, vital, pivotal, integral, essential

docs.title: Descriptions and States
docs.text:
• Various: a broad range of, a wide variety of
  • Improved: more fuel efficient, change in a better way
  • Modified: redesigned, change
  • Factual: statements, evidence, proof, testament, figures, statistics
  • Errors: inaccurate, inaccuracies, false, mistake, wrong, fault, incorrect, flaw, imperfection, defective
  • Discovery: finding, result, outcome, conclusion
  • Delicious: scrumptious, mouth-watering
  • Beautiful: breath-taking, spectacular, magnificent, picturesque
  • Modern (Society): contemporary

docs.title: Time and Frequency
docs.text:
• Happening soon: imminent
  • Everyday: on a day basis
  • Monthly: frequency month, each month, per month, every month, on a monthly basis
  • Yearly: frequency year, each year, per year, every year, on a yearly basis
  • Frequently (Regularly): on a frequent basis, on a regular basis

docs.title: Social and Environmental Contexts
docs.text:
• Society: communities
  • Thriving: prosperous, developed
  • Economic: financial
  • Key Element: foundation, keystone
  • Views: opinions, viewpoint, outlook, perspective, idea, conception, perception, assumption, belief, attitude, tone, notion
  • Lack of Money: financial constraint
  • Cheap: low-cost, inexpensive, affordable, reasonably priced
  • City People: urban citizens, residents, dwellers
  • Big City: major city, metropolitan areas, metropolises
  • People Living in Rural Areas: rural people, rural residents, countryside residents
  • Eco-Friendly: environmentally friendly
  • Young People: the young, young generation
  • Old People: elderly people, aged people, the old, the aged, the elderly
  • Social Networks: social media platforms, social networking sites, social media
  • Real-Life Experience: hands-on experience, real-world experience
  • Tourist Attraction: tourist spot, tourist destination
  • Job Opportunities: job prospects, career prospects, career chances
  • Perspective: viewpoints, outlook, opinion, idea, tune, attitude

docs.title: Challenges and Solutions
docs.text:
• Challenges: obstacles, difficulties
  • Solution: measure, approach, method, strategy

docs.title: Common Terms
docs.text:
• A lot of: much, many, a large number of, a large amount of
  • Friends: companions, peers, fellows
  • Hard-Working: diligent, industrious, assiduous
  • Mistakes: flaws, faults, imperfections, inaccuracies
  • Education: schooling
  • Delicious: scrumptious, mouth-watering

docs.title: Locating words
docs.text:
• Geographical location ⛰️
  • Name of an academic 🧪
  • History period ⏲️
  • Title of a publication 📰
  • Date of a study / survey 📅
  • Species / An animal 🐶
  • Terminology 📘

docs.title: Words with similar meanings (contextualized)
docs.text:
• Synomyms.
  • Part of speech (word form).

Example

The study by Canadian scientists in 2011 made a surprising discovery. \(\rightarrow\) "...and this was a finding that was completely unexpected." 

1. surprising ~ unexpected
2. discovery ~ finding

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
1. Pick the exact words in the text.
  2. Word limit (NO MORE THAN WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER).
  3. Part of Speech (Word form).
  4. Words with similar meanings (Synonyms).

Failure

  • Vượt số lượng từ cho phép.
  • Từ đã được paraphrase.

docs.title: The vibe
docs.text:
• Positive 👍
  • Negative 👎
  • Neutral 👐

docs.title: The theme
docs.text:
• Comparison \(\rightarrow\) comparison structures. 💁
  • Contrast \(\rightarrow\) constrast linking verb (However,...).
  • Money 💰
  • Physical aspects \(\rightarrow\) body. 👄👂👀
  • Brain \(\rightarrow\) neural,... 🧠

docs.title: Indicating time
docs.text:
• Present tense \(\rightarrow\) currently/present/at the moment
  • Early \(\rightarrow\) past/historic/ston age/medieval/century/V2/ed
  • Future \(\rightarrow\) upcoming/anticipations/long-term

docs.title: Noun
docs.text:
• Synonyms (contextualized):
    • Synonyms \(\rightarrow\) click here
    • Contextualized \(\rightarrow\) vocab.

    Example

    • change \(\rightarrow\) redesign, improve
    • effect (impact) \(\rightarrow\) increase/decrease
  • Generalization \(\rightarrow\) Specific objects.

Example

  • Innovative devices \(\rightarrow\) laptops, computers, cell phones,...
  • Science subjects \(\rightarrow\) Mathematics, Physics, Geogarphy,...

docs.title: Adjective
docs.text:
• Only synonyms.

Example

basic ~ fundamental ~ significant ~ radical

docs.title: Classification
docs.text:
Note

No highlight if all same type.

docs.title: SPEAKING
docs.text:
Info

  • Pronunciation \(\rightarrow\) 9
  • Fluency and Coherence \(\rightarrow\) 9
  • Lexical Resources (vocab) \(\rightarrow\) 9 \(\rightarrow\) topic-related vocab + academic language
  • Grammatical Range + Accuracy \(\rightarrow\) 9

docs.title: Choice question
docs.text:
Info

sample answers

docs.title: Do you like (prefer) A or B ?
docs.text:
• 1 of the 2 options
    • I would probably 😕 (definitely 💯) go for (opt for) paraphrasing / the former / the latter
    • I'm more inclined 😗 to V
    • I lean towards 😗 A/B
  • It depends
    • It kinda (sorta) depends 💁‍♂️
    • It depends (would depend) on + factor❗
    • If case 1 ➡️ A, If case 2 ➡️ B
  • Neither
    • Well, maybe (probably) neither of them 🙅‍♂️ ➡️ reasons + personal choice
    • Could I say neither of them? 😉 ➡️ reasons + personal choice

Note

  • Comparision / contrast language.
  • Discuss both sides.

docs.title: Idea development
docs.text:
• Like \(\rightarrow\) yes \(\rightarrow\) particular type \(\rightarrow\) reason (topic-related vocab + cohenrence) \(\rightarrow\) feeling / frequency.
  • Dislike \(\rightarrow\) no \(\rightarrow\) reason (related info / personality) \(\rightarrow\) general feelings / alternative (another choice).

Tip

  • Double tense (past - present).
  • Structure highlights:
    • (Not) have a strong liking (penchant) for
    • Be (not) really into + N/Ving
    • Be an (a) avid (big fan) of st
    • Be (not) an avid + Noun-er (reader, runner, traveler,...)
    • St be my Noun of choice
    • My favorite + Noun \(\rightarrow\) My go-to + Noun

Tip

  • Trick: Hobbies \(\rightarrow\) Present perfect.

docs.title: What do/don't you like about your hometown?
docs.text:
• PARTICULAR ASPECT.
  • Do \(\rightarrow\) There are quite a number of (several) things I (don't) really enjoy about , but (*)
    • (*): what I find most enjoyable is + N-Phrase (that SVO).
    • (*): what really annoys me all the time is + N-Phrase (that SVO).
  • Don't \(\rightarrow\) One particular thing I don't like about is . It's something that's been bugging me a lot recently. I wish + S + V2ed (could/would).

docs.title: What don't you like about your study?
docs.text:
• PARTICULAR ASPECT.
  • There are quite a number of things I dont really enjoy about my study, but what bugs me all the time is .

docs.title: Describe
docs.text:
Info

sample

docs.title: A job that you do not like to do in the future
docs.text:
• child 👶 ➡️ childhood dream ➡️ harbor a dream of Ving ➡️ ❓why❓
  • as I grow older 👨‍🦲 ➡️ change mindset ➡️ It's just dawned on me that ➡️ ❓reasons❓
  • be not born to become noun 🙅‍♂️
  • have no knack for Ving 💃
  • do not have (possess) what it takes to become noun 🙅‍♂️
  • be too adj (physically / mentally) demanding for me 💥

docs.title: An occasion when you heard someone complaining about something in a restaurant / store or other business places
docs.text:
• witness a significant complaint 👀
  • show dissatisfaction 💢
  • make a complaint 💬
  • insist on Ving 💢
  • tick (piss) someone off ➡️ ticked (pissed) me off ➡️ I got pissed off (nothing is more important than hygiene) 😠
  • make a fuss 📢
  • complain bitterly 🤦‍♂️
  • lose one's cool ➡️ shout at / yell at 🔊
  • offensive language 😡

docs.title: Noun 🧒
docs.text:
• Đếm được
    • Số ít ➡️ a/an + N.
    • Số nhiều ➡️ to be.
  • Không đếm được ➡️ to be.

docs.title: Verb 🏃
docs.text:
The same.

Note

Intransitive ➡️ V. Transitive ➡️ V + O.

docs.title: Adj 🥀 / V3ed (passive) 💥
docs.text:
• To be + Adj
  • Adj + Noun
  • Linking verb + Adj
  • make / find / keep + Adj
  • leave + O + Adj

Info

Word form tabular

docs.title: Tabular
docs.text: Noun Verb Adjective Adverb density densely tendency tend good well health healthy tiring tired addiction  addict addicted  addictive relaxation relaxed  relaxing

docs.title: Clause
docs.text: S + V + (O) - Verb: - Intransitive \(\rightarrow\) SV (E.g. Work). - Transitive \(\rightarrow\) SVO (E.g. Do).

docs.title: N-phrases
docs.text:
• Pre-modifier:
    • ADJ + N \(\rightarrow\) Characteristic.
    • N + N \(\rightarrow\) Featuring/Platform.
  • Post-modifier:
    • N + Ving/V3ed \(\rightarrow\) Reduced form of relative clause.

docs.title: Synonyms
docs.text:
• Condition:
    1. Part of speech
    2. Meaning

Example

  • State-of-the-art / Cutting-edge + N ➡️ Máy móc, công nghệ, thiết bị, dụng cụ, phát minh, ứng dụng, thiết kế tiên tiến, tối tân.
  • Contemporary + N ➡️ Suy nghĩa, điệu múa, âm nhạc, văn hoá, xã hội, lối sống, nền văn minh, con người.

docs.title: Part of speech / Word form
docs.text:
• Step 1. Define word having family words.
  • Step 2. Translate to Viet sentence obtained family words.
  • Step 3. Translate to Eng.

docs.title: Practice
docs.text:
TOPIC SENTENCE STRUCTURE POPULARITY 📈 - An increasing number of noun have a tendency to verb. - During the past few years, noun has become increasingly popular. - Over the last decades, noun has gained increasing popularity. 2 VIEWS 👀 - A school of thought holds that . - Some opine that . - Some are of the opinion that . - Some hold a firm belief that .

Note

Modal verb (should, could,...) ➡️ passive voice

docs.title: Chart type
docs.text:
• bar / column
  • line
  • pie
  • table

docs.title: Variable type
docs.text:
• time period
  • discrete categories

docs.title: Units
docs.text:
• , \(\rightarrow\) separation. e.g. 100,000,000
  • . \(\rightarrow\) decimal. e.g. 1.5

Example

5 thousand dollars, 300 million cars, 15.7%.

docs.title: Preposition
docs.text: Preposition Location Note Example from year x to year y - Subject Verb Adv + from x to y - There was a adj noun (key trend) + from x to y - categories witnessed (saw/ experenced) a adj noun (key trend) in subject + from x to y Adding specific year - from x in year X to y in year Y - from x to y between year X and year Y - The number of cars sold in the UK increased sharply from 10 million to 20 million cars between 1980 and 1985. TIME - during - over - in - throughout TIME + the x years -first - next - following - last bonus: 10 years \(\rightarrow\) a decade, 20 years \(\rightarrow\) 2 decades - UK witnessed a sighnificant increase in car sales from 5 million to 10 million cars in the first 5 years. at value point - start (begin/ stand)/ finish (end)/ peak + at value point - remained unchaged (stable/ constant) + at value point - don't miss V3/ed begin \(\rightarrow\) began - peak at = hit (reach) a peak of - In 1980, car sales in UK started at 10 million cars. to figure Verb Adv + to (only/merely) figure - Over the next 10 years, car sales decreased significantly to only 5 million cars in 1995. from figure x to figure y noun/verb + from x to y - Over the next 20 years, there was a significant drop from 30 million cars to merely 10 million cars in 1990. - Over the next 20 years, car sales declined sharply from 20 million cars to merely 5 million cars in 1995. by / of figure x to figure y - verb + by - noun + of - Apply for interval value. - by/of + approximately (roughly) x to (only/merely) y - Over the next 10 yeas, car sales declined sharply by roughly 15 million cars to merely 5 million cars in 1995. - Over the next 10 years, there was a significant drop of roughly 15 million cars to merely 5 million cars in 1995.

docs.title: Structure reducing
docs.text: Original Changing to Note Example Subject Verb_1 and Verb_2 Subject Verb_1, Verb_2-ing V1 and V2 is the same subject - In 1980, car sales in UK started at 10M cars, and then this figure rose sharply and peaked at 20M cars in 1985. -> In 1980, car sales in UK started at 10M cars, and then this figure rose sharply, peaking at 20M cars in 1985.

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text:
Abstract

  • Paraphrase the task.
    • Synonyms.
    • Part of speech \(\rightarrow\) word form.
  • Changing the sentence structures.

docs.title: Basic
docs.text: ORIGINAL CHANGING TO NOTE graph / diagram - the given - the provided : line graph, bar chart, column chart, pie chart below omitted show - illustrate - depict - compare - contrast don't miss s if several. information about data on in the subject under survey (not important) - time period \(\rightarrow\) : the changes - without time period \(\rightarrow\) : the differences from year to year - between and - over the course of years from to - during the period spanning from to in \(\rightarrow\) in the year

docs.title: Subject under survey
docs.text:
ORIGINAL CHANGING TO EXAMPLE data/figures - the number of noun (countable) + Ving/3ed - the amount of noun (uncountable) + Ving/3ed - the proportion (percentage/rate) of noun + Ving/3ed - the amount of money invested - the amount of money earned - the proportion of people using the local library - the amount of gas emitted from 2 different private vehicles. subject A in country B demonym B + subject A Children in Australia \(\rightarrow\) Australian children

Info

For another words \(\rightarrow\) synonyms

docs.title: Samples
docs.text: ORIGINAL CHANGED The table below shows information about the monthly investment of American government in education in 2010. The provided table illustrates (data on the differences in) the amount of money allocated for education on a monthly basis by the government in America in the year 2010. The line graph below shows the percentage of travelers who visited four different attractions in Brighton in 2009. The given line graph depicts the proportion of many of tourists visiting four different spots over the course of years from 2000 to 2010. The bar chart gives information about the yearly spending on healthcare service by the government in America from 1970 to 1980. The provided bar chart shows data on the amount of money spent per year on medical-care service by the government in the US during the period spanning from 1970 to 1980.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Abstract

Time period 
 - Changes (starting - ending point).
 - Comparison among the categories.
Discrete categories 
- Comparison among the categories.

Tip

Overview > 2 sentencesOverview = 1 sentence 
- Introduction 1 paragraph.
- Overview 1 paragraph.

- Introduction & overview 1 paragraph.

docs.title: Opening
docs.text:
• Overall, (it can be seen clearly from the chart)
  • Another highlight is that

docs.title: Key trends
docs.text:
TENDENCY VERB NOUN NOTE INCREASE - increase - rise - grow - go up - increase - rise - growth rise \(\rightarrow\) V2 \(\rightarrow\) rose DECREASE - decrease - decline - drop - fall - go down - decrease - decline - drop - fall - drop \(\rightarrow\) V2 \(\rightarrow\) dropped - fall \(\rightarrow\) V2 \(\rightarrow\) fell NO CHANGE - remain unchaged (stable/ constant) remain + almost 💯 (relatively 👀)

Warning

USING ALL V2 FOR KEY TRENDS !!!

docs.title: Describe adverb
docs.text:
STRONG WEAK - significantly - considerably - sharply - substantially - exponentially - noticeably - remarkably - slightly - moderately - marginally

Note

  • Intransitive verb \(\rightarrow\) adv + v | v + adv.
  • Transitive verb \(\rightarrow\) adv + VO | VO + adv.

docs.title: Structures
docs.text: STRUCTURE EXAMPLE Note Subject Verb Adv The number of cars produced by company A increase sharply during the surveyed period. There was a adj noun (key trend) in subject In UK, there was a marginal rise in the investment in public services. Adj is Adv bonus above ☝️ changed to Adj form. - categories witnessed (saw/ experenced) a adj noun (key trend) (in subject) - Subject witnessed (saw/ experienced) a adj noun (key trend) Beef consumption in England rose sharply, while France experienced a marginal fall throughout the surveyed period. - Maybe use when you see loop word status. - while / whereas Categories recorded (displayed) the highest/lowest subject - US recorded the highest proportion of spending on education. - Education in US recorded the highest proportion of spending. Subject 1 be (strong/weak) higher/lower than that in/on/at subject 2 - The proportion of students attending online classes at center A was much higher than that at center B. - UK men spent more time exercising than those in the US. - UK and US recorded a far higher proportion of spending on education than the other countries. - strong: significantly, considerably, far, much. - weak: slightly, a bit, a little. - that \(\rightarrow\) several \(\rightarrow\) those - subject 1 is the best \(\rightarrow\) than the other countries (the remaining countries).
